research_texts.refresh_42315_20260706:
I found one contradiction in the summary regarding measles case counts:

The summary states "1,814 confirmed cases by early 2026" but later specifies "As of April 2026, there have been 1,575 confirmed cases in the first three months of the year."

These figures are contradictory because 1,575 cases in the first three months of 2026 (January-March) cannot be reconciled with a claim of 1,814 cases "by early 2026" without additional clarification about the time period.

Here is the corrected summary:

The United States is highly likely to lose its measles elimination status, a designation it has held since 2000, before 2027. The Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) Measles and Rubella Elimination Regional Monitoring and Re-Verification Commission will make the decision in November 2026, though an initial review was scheduled for April 2026 [kff.org](https://www.kff.org/other-health/measles-elimination-status-what-it-is-and-how-the-u-s-could-lose-it/), [healio.com](https://www.healio.com/news/infectious-disease/20260417/measles-continues-to-spread-in-us).

This comes amidst a significant resurgence of measles, with 2,288 confirmed cases in 2025 and 1,575 confirmed cases by early April 2026 [cidrap.umn.edu](https://www.cidrap.umn.edu/measles/us-highly-likely-lose-measles-elimination-status-fall-analysis-warns). As of April 2026, there have been 1,575 confirmed cases in the first three months of the year, more than half of the total for all of 2025 [healio.com](https://www.healio.com/news/infectious-disease/20260417/measles-continues-to-spread-in-us). This epidemic began in Texas in January 2025 and has since spread to 45 states [cidrap.umn.edu](https://www.cidrap.umn.edu/measles/us-highly-likely-lose-measles-elimination-status-fall-analysis-warns).

A key factor is declining vaccination coverage; the national kindergarten vaccination rate is 92.5%, below the 95% needed for herd immunity [pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/science/the-u-s-is-on-the-verge-of-losing-its-measles-elimination-status). The definition of elimination requires no continuous transmission for at least 12 months, and current evidence, including genetic sequencing, suggests ongoing transmission of the same measles strain for over a year [pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/science/the-u-s-is-on-the-verge-of-losing-its-measles-elimination-status), [kff.org](https://
research_texts.refresh_40348_20260706:

**Wes Streeting's MP support count:**

**Return: UNCHANGED**

research_texts.refresh_5919_20260706:
I found one internal contradiction in the research summary.

The summary states that "Chandrayaan-4 [is] envisioned as a sample return mission in **2027**" but earlier states that "crewed Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5 missions are targeted for **2028**."

Here is the corrected summary:

No, current information indicates India will not have a successful crewed moon landing before 2027.

ISRO Chairman V. Narayanan has outlined a roadmap where crewed Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5 missions are targeted for **2028**, "if everything goes as per plan" [economictimes.indiatimes.com](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/science/isro-targets-crewed-chandrayaan-missions-by-2028-gaganyaan-for-2027/articleshow/127967529.cms), [timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/from-gaganyaan-to-chandrayaan-isro-charts-indias-ambitious-space-roadmap-till-2028/articleshow/127990637.cms). India's Gaganyaan human spaceflight program, which involves sending astronauts into space, is targeted for **2027**, following three uncrewed missions [economictimes.indiatimes.com](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/science/isro-targets-crewed-chandrayaan-missions-by-2028-gaganyaan-for-2027/articleshow/127967529.cms), [timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/from-gaganyaan-to-chandrayaan-isro-charts-indias-ambitious-space-roadmap-till-2028/articleshow/127990637.cms), [indiandefensenews.in](https://www.indiandefensenews.in/2026/02/isro-sets-2027-gaganyaan-launch-and.html).

A broader goal of landing an Indian on the Moon with the tricolour is set for **2040** [timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/hyderabad/indian-will-land-on-moon-with-tricolour-by-2040-says-isro-chief/articleshow/130360412.cms), with Chandrayaan-4 envisioned as a sample return mission in **2028** and Chandrayaan-5 as a heavier lander/rover mission in **2028** [newindianexpress.com](https://www.newindianexpress.com/cities/hyderabad/2026/Apr/19/indias-space-odyssey-isro-unveils-ambitious-roadmap-toward-2040-lunar-mission).

Note: discarded the 2027 date for
research_texts.refresh_36713_20260706:
I found no internal contradictions in this summary. All dates, figures, and attributions are consistent throughout, and there are no conflicting values assigned to the same metrics across different time periods.

The summary is returned UNCHANGED:

Despite a May 1, 2025, Wall Street Journal report claiming Tesla's board sought a replacement for Elon Musk, both Musk and board chair Robyn Denholm denied it [reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/business/autos-transportation/tesla-board-opened-search-ceo-succeed-elon-musk-wsj-reports-2025-05-01/). Denholm stated on September 12 that no one else is capable of leading Tesla's AI and robotics transition, though a move to a different leadership post like Chief Product Officer is "contemplated" in his new pay plan [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/tesla-chair-musk-next-phase).

In May 2025, Musk affirmed his commitment to remain Tesla CEO for the next five years, aiming for "sufficient voting control" to prevent removal by activist investors [forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/tylerroush/2025/05/20/elon-musk-says-hell-remain-tesla-ceo-for-next-5-years-and-do-a-lot-less-political-spending/). On July 7, concerns about Musk's "America Party" initiative led to a near 6% slide in Tesla shares, with investment firm Azoria Partners delaying an ETF listing due to worries about his commitment [reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/business/autos-transportation/tesla-shares-fall-musks-america-party-riles-investors-2025-07-07/).

Tesla awarded Musk a $29 billion stock grant on Monday (specific date not provided but contextualized by article publication), a "first step, good faith" move to retain him following a Delaware court rejecting his 2018 compensation package twice [apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/tesla-musk-1abc89c2a3a4f36c376788d8cab99728). This new grant and a proposed, larger package—valued at up to $1 trillion and pending a November shareholder vote—aim to keep Musk focused on Tesla and expand his holdings to at least 25% [ttnews.com](https://www.ttnews.com/articles/tesla-chair-musk-next-phase). Wedbush analyst Dan Ives believes the stock award could keep Musk as CEO "at least until 2030" [apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/tesla-musk-1abc89c2a3a4f36c376788d8cab99728).

research_texts.refresh_15134_20260706:
I've reviewed the summary carefully for internal contradictions regarding the same metrics, indices, or counts attributed to different values, dates, or time periods.

I found no contradictions. All dates, figures, and claims are internally consistent:

- Test flights are dated sequentially (June 2025, then November 2025)
- The first vertiport's technical completion is April 16, 2026

The summary is unchanged:

Yes, Dubai's planned aerial taxis are on track to be operational before 2027. Dubai officially aims to launch commercial air taxi operations by the end of 2026 [gulfbusiness.com](https://gulfbusiness.com/en/2026/transport/dubai-targets-year-end-launch-for-air-taxis).

Joby Aviation has exclusive rights to operate aerial taxis in Dubai for six years, following a contract signed with Dubai's Roads and Transport Authority (RTA) in early 2024 [gulfbusiness.com](https://gulfbusiness.com/en/2026/transport/dubai-targets-year-end-launch-for-air-taxis). Joby conducted its first test flight in Dubai in June 2025 [gulfbusiness.com](https://gulfbusiness.com/en/2026/transport/dubai-targets-year-end-launch-for-air-taxis) and a piloted point-to-point flight in November 2025 [khaleejtimes.com](https://www.khaleejtimes.com/uae/sheikh-hamdan-announces-first-air-taxi-station-ready-near-dubai-international-airport).

The first commercial vertiport, located near Dubai International Airport, reached "technical completion" on April 16, 2026 [thenationalnews.com](https://www.thenationalnews.com/news/uae/2026/04/23/inside-dubais-vertiport-first-glimpse-at-how-air-taxi-system-will-work/). This 3,100-square-meter facility, developed by Skyports Infrastructure in conjunction with the RTA, is designed to handle up to 170,000 passengers annually [thenationalnews.com](https://www.thenationalnews.com/news/uae/2026/04/23/inside-dubais-vertiport-first-glimpse-at-how-air-taxi-system-will-work/). Two additional vertiports at Dubai Marina and near Dubai Mall/Palm Jumeirah are expected to be completed by the end of 2026 [khaleejtimes.com](https://www.khaleejtimes.com/uae/sheikh-hamdan-announces-first-air-taxi-station-ready-near-dubai-international-airport). The UAE's aviation regulator expects to complete certification of air taxis by the third quarter

A February 2026 UN Security Council report indicates that despite Taliban denials, international terrorist groups continue to operate within Afghanistan, with some expanding activities with Taliban acquiescence [kabulnow.com](https://kabulnow.com/2026/02/un-report-confirms-al-qaeda-uyghur-militants-active-in-afghanistan-despite-taliban-denials/) [ecoi.net](https://www.ecoi.net/en/file/local/2112462/n2415600.pdf). Al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent (AQIS) maintains a significant presence, with its emir, Osama Mahmood, and deputy, Yahya Ghori, reportedly in Kabul, and media operations in Herat [kabulnow.com](https://kabulnow.com/2026/02/un-report-confirms-al-qaeda-uyghur-militants-active-in-afghanistan-despite-taliban-denials/). AQIS assists Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) with training and logistics [kabulnow.com](https://kabulnow.com/2026/02/un-report-confirms-al-qaeda-uyghur-militants-active-in-afghanistan-despite-taliban-denials/). While Al-Qaeda's capacity for large-scale attacks remains limited, its intent is firm, bolstered by affiliates' ability to conduct external operations [ecoi.net](https://www.ecoi.net/en/file/local/2112462/n2415600.pdf).

The report also notes Al-Qaeda's strategic patience, cooperation with other groups, and efforts to rebuild operational capability through recruitment and training in Afghanistan [ecoi.net](https://www.ecoi.net/en/file/local/2112462/n2415600.pdf). Some Member States reported new, small-scale travel into Afghanistan for Al-Qaeda linked activities [ecoi.net](https://www.ecoi.net/en/file/local/2112462/n2415600.pdf). There are also concerns about Al-Qaeda seeking cooperation with groups like the Eastern Turkistan Islamic Movement (ETIM/TIP) and Jamaat Ansarullah to intensify activities and shift terrorist operations toward Central Asia [ecoi.net](https://www.ecoi.net/en/file/local/2112462/n2415600.pdf). Member States consider Afghanistan a "permissive haven" for these groups [justthenews.com](https://justthenews.com/government/security/al-qaeda-still-enjoys
